pages.name: The Eleventh Bell Transfer — Setup & Read-Aloud

text.content:
The Eleventh Bell Transfer

The climax of Phase 6 takes place in the Old Stonecistern, a disused civic water cistern beneath a derelict bathhouse in the Foreign Quarter. The cistern's service drains connect to the Brass Crow's back alley.

This is not the Underdark. It is Greyhawk infrastructure: pre-Reformation, partially flooded, partially walled off, used by no one official. It feels like a rehearsal for descent without being one.

What happens here

At the eleventh bell on the night the party identifies the transfer (or, if the Trail Clock is past 4, on the night Korre has rescheduled), the following figures arrive to conduct the transfer:

  • Haskur Vandrell — brought down from his Brass Crow guest room by Korre's two agents. He carries a small case of papers and a sealed packet (the Ashen Itinerary's pre-burning state).
  • Kethren Ilvath — arrives from the cistern's other side, alone. He will brief Haskur, take possession of the packet's “second page,” and lead Haskur out through the deeper drain that the party cannot yet follow.
  • Korre's two agents — Mell and Saern. They will burn what does not travel.
  • V. Korre — only if the Trail Clock is 0–2 and the Heat Clock is 0–2. Otherwise she sends a proxy.

The conspiracy's goals (in order of priority)

  1. Burn the packet (the Ashen Itinerary).
  2. Move Haskur out through the deeper drain.
  3. Let Kethren escape with the second page.
  4. Erase the route mark scratched into the cistern wall.

The conspiracy does not want to fight the party. Its goal is to escape clean. If forced into combat, every NPC will pursue their priority before they pursue damage.

How the party reaches the cistern

Once the party identifies the eleventh-bell transfer (any three primary nodes), they know the location:

  • From Korre's apartment: the abbreviation “OBT” resolves to Old Bath, Tinker's. The disused bathhouse is at the south end of Tinker's Way, three streets past the Brass Crow.
  • From the Brass Crow registry: the phrase “rain door” identifies the cellar service entrance — a low door under the bathhouse's broken portico, accessible from the alley.
  • From Vone's irregular ledger: the “old bath, Tinker's Way side” notation pinpoints the cistern.
  • From Iren (if shown the “OBT” abbreviation): she identifies the Old Stonecistern from her own civic-records knowledge in two minutes.

Read aloud — approach to the bathhouse

The bathhouse stands on the south end of Tinker's Way where the street starts to slope toward the river. It has been derelict for a generation. Slate has come off the roof in patches; the front portico has lost two columns and is held up by a leaning wooden brace; the door has been planked shut and is now half-rotten.

The alley around the side is narrower than it looks on the surveyors' map. Down a flight of slick stone steps below the portico's broken corner, a low iron door is set into the cellar wall. Above the door, almost flush with the lintel, someone has scratched a small mark in the soot: seven steps cut under a black hill. The mark is fresh.

Read aloud — entering

The iron door yields. Beyond it the air changes: stone-cool, mineral-damp, the faint sweetness of standing water. A short passage, three uneven steps, and then the cistern itself opens out below you.

It is larger than expected. The dry platform — old bath tiles, cracked, stained — slopes down to a wet lower channel of green-black water. Light reaches the room only from a ceiling grate to the street far above, where the streetlamp flickers like bad coin on the surface of the water. There is a brazier set in the brick floor at the far end of the platform, lit, the iron sides streaked with old soot.

You are not the first ones here this evening.

Encounter approach options

Even at the climax, combat is not the only solution. Use whichever the party sets up.

  • Shadow and observe — if the party arrived early enough (Stealth DC 14, or hidden by the cistern's columns), they can watch the transfer happen, identify everyone present, and intervene at a chosen moment or not at all.
  • Ambush — the party prepares the room before the conspiracy arrives. Surprise round and a likely capture if combat is forced.
  • Social interruption — one PC reveals themselves and offers Korre a deal in front of the agents. Risky but legitimate; Korre is paying attention.
  • Legal seizure — if the party brought a Greyhawk advocate or summoned the Watch (with risk), they can interrupt under colour of law. The conspiracy attempts to flee rather than resist; the packet may still burn.
  • Magical surveillance — clairvoyance, scrying, or similar yields the location and the players. Useful for staging an ambush.
  • Direct fight — the party walks in. Combat begins. This is the GM's last preference, not their first.

Combat triggers

Combat starts only if:

  • A PC is identified entering the cistern by an NPC who has line of sight (Mell on the platform, Saern at the brazier).
  • A PC tries to take the packet directly without setting up.
  • The party announces themselves loudly.
  • Kethren sees a PC he recognises from earlier in the phase.

If combat is not triggered, the party may shadow Kethren out through the deeper drain (he goes through a sluice gate the party cannot follow), and intercept Haskur at the cistern's south side as he is being moved toward a courier wagon. This is the cleanest capture.

Old Stonecistern — Keyed Map Brief

Brief for future battlemap generation. No image required for play; the GM may sketch as desired. Footprint: roughly 70 ft east–west by 40 ft north–south, irregular, with the dry platform on the south side and the wet channel along the north.

Mood

  • Slick stone underfoot.
  • Green-black water, surface broken only when something moves through it.
  • Old bath tiles, fragments still glazed.
  • Mineral smell, faintly sweet.
  • Iron grates rusting at the joints.
  • Muffled street noise overhead through one ceiling grate.
  • Torchlight reflecting like bad coin on the water.

Keyed locations

#FeatureNotes
1Upper cellar entranceThe iron “rain door” from the alley. Three uneven steps in.
2Broken bathhouse stairA stone stair on the south wall, partly collapsed, leading up to the bathhouse main floor. Climbable (DC 12); not safe (DC 14 to descend silently).
3Dry cistern platformThe southern half of the room, raised about 18 inches above the channel. Old tiles, broken columns. Most of the action happens here.
4Wet lower channelThe north side, knee- to chest-deep depending on placement. Difficult terrain. Visibility downward is none.
5Old sluice gateAt the east end of the channel. Iron lattice; sealed by a maintenance lock. Kethren has the key. This is the deeper drain — the route the party cannot follow.
6Pipe bridgeA 24-inch-wide pipe section crosses the channel near the centre, at platform height. Used as a footbridge. DC 11 Acrobatics to cross at speed; DC 8 to walk normally.
7Packet-burning brazierAn iron brazier on the platform's far end. Coals are already lit when the party arrives. The Ashen Itinerary will be burned here unless interrupted.
8Narrow maintenance crawlA 30-inch crawlspace at the south wall, west end, leads up to the bathhouse roof. DC 12 Athletics; useful for a flanking PC.
9Locked service door to alleyAt the west end of the platform. DC 15 lock; opens to an alley behind the cobbler's. Korre's escape route in worst-case.
10Deep water pocketA drop in the channel near the centre — chest-deep becomes 8 ft deep. Anyone going under takes one round to surface. Useful for hiding; risky.
11Ceiling grate to street aboveThe only natural light source. Audible street noise. Throwing voice up the grate is an option.
12Hidden route-mark scratched into old stoneA small mark on the platform's south wall, beside the broken stair: seven descending cuts beneath a hill silhouette. The route mark for the Seventh Stair. Kethren is here to confirm and refresh it.
13Escape channel (sealed)At the west end of the channel, a heavily grated drain. This leads to a sealed city drain, not yet to the deep route. The party can investigate it later; for tonight, it is not the way out.

Tactical notes

  • Light: dim throughout. The brazier is bright light in a 10-ft radius; the ceiling grate gives dim light in a 20-ft radius. Everything else is darkness.
  • Cover: the broken columns and the broken stair give half cover at platform level; the channel surface gives no cover but does give concealment.
  • Difficult terrain: the entire wet channel.
  • Choke points: the upper cellar entrance, the pipe bridge, the sluice gate.
  • Sound: the cistern carries voices. A whisper at the platform's south end is audible (Perception 13) at the channel's east end.

What makes the space playable

The cistern works because it has three distinct fight modes: the dry platform (skirmish + missile), the wet channel (slow, dangerous, hide-and-seek), and the verticals (broken stair, ceiling grate, maintenance crawl). The party can choose which mode they fight in, and which mode the conspiracy is forced into. Reward planning. A party that locks the alley door before engaging cuts off Korre's escape; a party that puts a PC at the sluice gate before Kethren can flee can hold him.

Optional Combat Encounter

Combat at the cistern is optional. If it happens, this is the encounter. Adjust by clock state and party level.

Combatants

Haskur Vandrell — modified Mage operator (CR 6)

  • HP: 64. AC: 13. Speed: 30 ft.
  • Saves: INT +6, WIS +4. Skills: Arcana +6, Deception +5, Persuasion +5.
  • Spells (CHA-flavor caster, save DC 14, +6 to hit): cantrips: fire bolt, mage hand, minor illusion, prestidigitation; 1st: shield, mage armor (cast pre-fight); 2nd: misty step × 2 prepared, suggestion; 3rd: counterspell, fly; 4th: dimension door (one cast remaining).
  • Reaction: counterspell (3rd level). Tactics: opens with fly if available, then misty step away as needed. Will not blast. Will suggestion a PC to leave the platform. Uses dimension door to escape only if HP is below half.

Kethren Ilvath — Deep-Route Courier (CR 5)

  • HP: 58. AC: 16 (light armor + dex). Speed: 35 ft, climb 30 ft.
  • Saves: DEX +7, WIS +4. Skills: Acrobatics +7, Perception +4, Stealth +9.
  • Senses: passive Perception 14. Treat as having keen low-light vision; the GM may describe him as comfortable in dim conditions without confirming origin.
  • Multiattack: makes two melee attacks per round (poison needle + short blade), or one attack and Disengage as a bonus action.
  • Poison Needle: melee weapon attack, +7 to hit, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1d4+4 piercing + DC 13 CON save or poisoned for 1 minute (save end of turn).
  • Short Blade: melee weapon attack, +7 to hit, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1d6+4 piercing.
  • Reaction: Defensive Step — when targeted by a melee attack, may move 5 ft as a reaction without provoking; the attack has disadvantage.
  • Bonus Actions: Disengage; Hide; Vanish (3/day) — when in dim light or darkness, becomes invisible until next attack or end of next turn.
  • Tactics: priorities are (1) get the second page from Haskur, (2) burn the packet, (3) escape through the sluice gate. Will fight to delay only. Uses Vanish aggressively. Will not pursue.

Korre's two agents — Mell & Saern (CR 1 each)

Treat as Spy (MM 2024) for Mell and Thug (MM 2024) for Saern. Mell handles intimidation and lock work; Saern handles the brazier and the burning. Saern's combat priority is the brazier — he will move directly there and start burning the packet within two rounds.

V. Korre — Spy Master analog (CR 4–5)

Present only if Trail Clock 0–2 and Heat Clock 0–2.

  • HP: 52. AC: 14 (studded leather + dex). Speed: 30 ft.
  • Skills: Deception +6, Insight +5, Stealth +6. Saves: DEX +5, WIS +4, CHA +6.
  • Multiattack: shortsword + hand crossbow, OR shortsword + Cunning Action (Disengage / Hide).
  • Cunning Action: Dash, Disengage, or Hide as a bonus action.
  • Reaction (Parry): when targeted by a melee attack, +2 AC against that attack.
  • Tactics: Korre does not stand and fight. She gives orders, retrieves the packet, and uses the locked service door (room 9) to flee. If she is caught with the packet on her person, the party has won the phase.

Enemy morale

  • Haskur: flees if bloodied or if Kethren commands him to. Will not die for the conspiracy.
  • Kethren: prioritises packet destruction and escape. Fights only as much as needed to buy seconds. If Kethren is reduced below half HP, he uses Vanish + sluice gate exit. He cannot be caught at the gate without a PC blocking it before round 2.
  • Korre's agents: buy time. Saern dies for the brazier; Mell does not.
  • Korre: flees before fighting to the death. She will burn records before fighting.

No one fights to the death unless cornered. The conspiracy is moving information, not building heroic last stands. Reward the party for closing exits.

Victory conditions

Any one of these is enough to continue:

  • Capture Haskur.
  • Capture Kethren.
  • Capture Korre.
  • Recover the Ashen Itinerary intact.
  • Recover the route mark (rubbing or sketch).
  • Prevent the packet from burning.
  • Track the deeper-drain escape route — even just to the next sealed grate.

Failure conditions (and what survives)

Even total tactical failure leaves at least one clue:

  • The route mark “VII Stair” on the cistern wall (rubbing or sketch).
  • A wax flake on the platform or the alley.
  • A scorched fragment of the Ashen Itinerary, with the phrase “no sail, no road” still legible.
  • Iren's later reading, if the party brings her any of the above.

Chase rules (if Haskur or Kethren flees through the cistern)

Use a simple opposed structure: each round, fleeing NPC and pursuing PC each roll Athletics or Acrobatics (PC's choice; NPC uses better stat). Higher result moves 30 ft toward their goal. Ties hold position. Environmental hazards apply each round:

  • Wet channel: difficult terrain (DC 11 Acrobatics or fall prone in water).
  • Pipe bridge: DC 11 Acrobatics to cross at full speed; failure costs 10 ft.
  • Sluice gate: locked. Kethren has the key; pursuing PCs need DC 16 to pick or DC 17 to force.
  • Maintenance crawl: 30-inch height; DC 12 Athletics; weapons larger than a shortsword cannot be used inside.
  • Locked service door (room 9): DC 15.

Kethren's goal is the sluice gate. Haskur's goal is the locked service door (which Mell unlocks for him as he goes through). Korre's goal is also the locked service door, two seconds behind Haskur.

If the party manages to block any one of these — a PC at the sluice gate, a PC at the alley door, a PC at the broken stair — the chase resolves with at least one capture, depending on which exit the party held.

Scaling for Party Level

Phase 6 assumes a level 8–11 party. The cistern encounter is built at the bottom of that range; scale up for the top.

Level 8

  • Haskur as written (CR 6).
  • Kethren as written (CR 5).
  • Mell + Saern (CR 1 each).
  • Korre present only if clocks 0–2.

Level 9–10

  • Haskur gains a third level of misty step casting and one prepared banishment (4th-level slot used).
  • Kethren's HP +12; gains Misdirection (when targeted, can swap places with a willing or unwitting ally within 5 ft as a reaction; ally is hit instead).
  • Add one more agent (Mell-equivalent).
  • Korre present at clocks 0–3.

Level 11

  • Haskur upgrades to Archmage-lite: AC 14, HP 96, adds fireball (used for control, not damage), greater invisibility (one cast, used to escape).
  • Kethren upgrades to CR 7: HP 70, gains Phantom Step (1/short rest, takes the Dodge and Disengage action together as a single bonus action; movement does not provoke).
  • Add two more agents (one Mell, one Saern; total four).
  • Korre is present regardless of clock state.

Difficulty controls

If the encounter is going too easily for the party, the GM should not buff the NPCs in mid-fight. Instead:

  • Have the City Watch arrive at the ceiling grate two rounds in. The party may now be fighting and trying not to be detained.
  • Have a fresh courier wagon back into the alley, blocking room 9. Korre and Haskur may now both reach it at the same time.
  • Bring up the deep water pocket (room 10) as a hazard for any PC who enters the channel.

If the encounter is going too hard:

  • Saern panics and lights the brazier early; the smoke gives partial concealment to the party as well.
  • Mell breaks for the alley door early, leaving the platform under-defended.
  • Haskur misses with a key spell (the dice are real; do not fudge, but do not stack).

The point of the cistern is not to win or lose a fight. The point is to take or fail to take a man, and to read or fail to read a route mark. Both can happen in many combinations. Let the dice tell the story; the campaign continues either way.
